page: Amazon Web Services g6e.12xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For accelerated workloads, it bundles four NVIDIA L40S GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a total of 179 GB of VRAM.

Local storage consists of a 3800 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance supports a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. The AMD EPYC processor includes hardware-level features such as AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA_ni cryptographic extensions, alongside BMI2, ADX, and FMA for integer and bit manipulation. It lacks AVX-512 extensions and AMX matrix instructions.

In benchmark testing, the g6e.12xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in large language model inference, processing prompts and generating text in the top 10% across 135M, 7B, and 70B parameter models. CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, as well as PassMark CPU and encryption tests. Ray tracing and image processing benchmarks also register in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth and general database operations perform within the average range.

Given the integration of four enterprise-grade GPUs, high-speed local NVMe storage, and 100 Gbps networking, this instance offers high resource density for specialized workloads. It is designed for LLM inference, graphics-intensive rendering, ray tracing, and cryptographic processing. The hardware profile represents a qualitative trade-off, prioritizing GPU acceleration and high-speed local storage over maximum memory bandwidth.
description: Amazon Web Services g6e.12xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Acceleration is driven by four NVIDIA L40S Ada Lovelace GPUs with 179 GB of total VRAM. Storage is provided by a 3800 GB local NVMe SSD, complemented by a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark evaluations, the instance achieves top-tier performance in LLM inference across small, medium, and large models. It also registers strong scores in CPU stress tests, encryption, ray tracing, and image processing, while memory bandwidth and database operations remain in the average tier. This hardware configuration is suited for large language model deployment, graphics rendering, and cryptographic workloads requiring high-speed local storage and GPU acceleration.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.12xlarge features 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, four NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 179 GB VRAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference and ray tracing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, four NVIDIA L40S GPUs, and 3800 GB of NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated platform featuring four NVIDIA L40S GPUs, local NVMe storage, and 100 Gbps networking for demanding machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with four NVIDIA L40S GPUs providing 179 GB of total VRAM.
Features 48 vCPUs via AMD EPYC 7R13 processors and 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory.
Includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Delivers top-tier performance in LLM inference for various model sizes.
Achieves strong benchmarks in ray tracing, image processing, and CPU encryption.
Optimized for large language model deployment, graphics rendering, and cryptographic tasks.
